description: Provenance for the committed FOCUS 1.4 machine-readable model (focus_1_4_model.json). The JSON is a deterministic extraction of the FinOps Foundation FOCUS 1.4 Data Model and is the artifact of record for this package.

source.name: FinOps FOCUS 1.4 Data Model workbook
source.artifact: FOCUS 1.4 Data Model workbook (spreadsheet), downloaded from https://focus.finops.org; not redistributed in this repository

supplements.description: ServiceSubcategory -> parent Category map, transcribed from the FOCUS v1.4 specification; an input to the extractor.

generator.runtime: Dev-time only; requires openpyxl. Neither the runtime package nor the test suite imports openpyxl — the committed JSON is the artifact of record.
generator.serialization: json.dumps(model, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + trailing newline; UTF-8. Deterministic: identical (workbook, supplement, generator) reproduce identical output bytes.

reproducibility.byte_for_byte: The committed output is byte-reproducible by re-running the pinned generator on the same source workbook: the serialization is deterministic (sorted keys, fixed indent, UTF-8, trailing newline).
verifiable_in_ci: supplements[].sha256 match the committed supplement files
reproducibility.limitation: None for the model chain: the source workbook is hashed (source.artifact_sha256) and the committed output was reproduced byte-for-byte from it by the pinned extractor at completion time. The workbook itself is still not redistributed; obtain it from the FinOps Foundation and verify it against source.artifact_sha256.

attribution: FOCUS(TM) is a trademark of the FinOps Foundation. The FOCUS specification and data model are (c) the FinOps Foundation and are licensed CC-BY-4.0. The committed model JSON and this provenance file are a derivative work distributed under CC-BY-4.0 with attribution. This project is an independent community toolkit and is not endorsed by the FinOps Foundation.
notes: provenance_status was set to 'complete' by scripts/verify_model_provenance.py --complete: the source workbook was hashed and the committed model was reproduced byte-for-byte from it with the pinned extractor.
